Belia.notes: Both suffix and base meanings are unknown , thus compound translation is questionable

Githli.notes: Both suffix and base meanings are unknown , thus compound translation is questionable

Ivkhra.notes: It comes from 'Khra' conjunction's meaning, I'm not sure if it is valid...

Kagithli.notes: It is just a proposal, I am not sure if it even makes sense. Translation is open for discussion

Kajahu.notes: Propably not very useful... but reasonable

Kakhra.notes: It is just a proposal, I am not sure if it even makes sense. Translation is open for discussion

Kaoth.notes: It is just a proposal, I am not sure if it even makes sense. Translation is open for discussion

Kaoull.notes: The 'Oull' has no clear definiton of ''possibility' - is it single option? Space of countless options?

Kaozun.notes: I have trouble with defining inversion/oppositon of dust, that is the most reasonable proposal i came up with

Karanac.notes: As base word translation is uncertain, so is this compound's.

Khra.notes: 'Khra' is also a conjunction, with 'the time has come for X' meening

Mahk.notes: Wiki mentions 2 words with 'Behold' meaning. It's not certain which one is correct...

Ranac.notes: Not confirmed , but assumed true in face of no alternatives

Shogrikh.notes: 'Great strength' meaning has been added by me , it is not listed in wiki, but as 'strength' is one of 'Grikh' meaning, I found it important to add it.

Tarat.notes: Extracted from shotarat - as lord is lesser then overlord/master, I belive it makes sense

Vagithullah.notes: Va means 'claw', but the rest of compound components is unknown and it is hard to guess how they together create the final meaning

Voc.notes: Extracted from Voca - as game description describes Voca as 'voices/screams' , I deducted that Voc would be singular 'voice/scream'. Additionaly, it gave me an idea for plural noun suffix (-a), which I will use until I find more suitable replacement
